samples.reason: Human reviewer can make a quick scope decision now; only proceed to deeper review if program policy includes learning hubs or if selecting specific subprojects for independent screening.
samples.title: ai-engineering-hub

samples.reason: High-readability candidate for quick human triage: classify first as resource hub, then split out any truly productized subprojects if needed.
samples.title: awesome-llm-apps

samples.reason: Repository appears to be a concrete AI application (Streamlit RAG app) with deploy/run instructions and configurable runtime behavior. It is more than a concept note, but human review should verify production maturity, maintenance activity, and whether it meets inclusion thresholds beyond a demo/reference implementation.
samples.title: rag

samples.reason: High-value next step: inspect repository tree and recent commits for actual implementation modules (agent/tool orchestration, code edit pipeline, scaffold generation) before any promotion decision.
samples.title: copilot

samples.reason: Repository combines two tracks (general chatbot/model-training + ShoppingAgent product workflow). Evidence shows practical AI productization signals, but scope is broad and partly roadmap-oriented, so classification risk is non-trivial.

samples.reason: Needs a quick taxonomy decision first: include as AI app framework candidate or exclude from chatbot slice as supporting infrastructure.

samples.reason: Ready for quick human first-pass: likely exclude from active candidate pool unless reviewer is intentionally collecting legacy/reference chatbot implementations.

samples.reason: If in doubt, split into two records for human review: (1) this repo as a curated ecosystem index, (2) Composio/connect-apps as a potential productized automation platform.

samples.reason: Strong fit as AI-agent memory infrastructure with concrete productization signals (SDK/API, hosted + self-hosted, integrations), but performance claims are self-reported and should be verified before candidate_pool promotion.
samples.title: mem0

samples.reason: Repository strongly presents as a productized, self-hosted personal AI assistant with broad channel integrations and onboarding. However, evidence is mostly self-description; independent validation of maturity, single-user vs team scope, and practical deployability is still needed.

samples.reason: Human reviewer should quickly inspect repo activity metrics, release artifacts, enterprise docs/screens, and any independent usage signals before deciding candidate_pool inclusion.
samples.title: NextChat

samples.reason: Repository emphasizes deployment, camera ingest, recording, streaming, encryption, and integrations. AI/ML is only referenced as a possible capability in the broader Kerberos.io ecosystem, not clearly as core functionality of this specific repo.

samples.reason: Repository appears to be a production-oriented RAG reference solution/blueprint with deployment, governance, and operations details, but final inclusion should confirm whether the observatory scope prefers end-user products vs. developer reference stacks.

Interpret the Phase1 Exit Checklist conflict: confirm whether the existing GitHub live matrix counts toward the GitHub full-cycle gate and whether the Product Hunt full-cycle line stays deferred outside the current release.

rationale: GitHub remains the only current-phase live candidate discovery path, matching the frozen source boundary.
Product Hunt remains deferred in the current phase, while the official GraphQL plus token-auth live seam stays preserved for future reactivation.
Local mart-backed dashboard reconciliation currently passes all materialized checks.
Merge spot-check evidence is not materialized in the local baseline, so the absence of sampled merge-risk cases cannot be treated as a completed release audit.
The Phase1 exit checklist still contains a Product Hunt full-cycle requirement that conflicts with the frozen deferred boundary, so release interpretation remains owner-gated.
DEC-025 keeps final merge/release sign-off with the owner, so this report can only provide a machine judgment plus required owner decisions.
release_conditions: Phase1 Exit Checklist still lists a full Product Hunt collection cycle, while the frozen current-phase boundary keeps Product Hunt in fixture/replay/contract mode only.
